scenario: Tessera Banking runs a multi-tenant Camunda 8.8 platform with 9 Spring Boot services serving 30 tenants and 17000 jobs per hour. The team sets tenant-ids under camunda.client.worker.defaults for the global fleet, but one job type must serve only 2 of those tenants via an override.
question: How does tenant-ids under camunda.client.worker.override behave relative to the defaults list per the 8.8 reference?

options.text: The 8.8 reference documents that tenant-ids under override replaces the list of tenant IDs from the annotation for the keyed job type at registration.
The 8.8 reference documents that tenant-ids under override extends the list of tenant IDs from the annotation for the keyed job type at registration.
The 8.8 reference documents that tenant-ids under override intersects the list of tenant IDs from the annotation for the keyed job type at registration.
The 8.8 reference documents that tenant-ids under override is ignored if the annotation already lists tenant IDs for the keyed job type at registration.

a.text: Correct. The 8.8 reference says tenant-ids under override replaces the list of tenant IDs, mirroring how fetch-variables behaves between defaults and override scopes.

b.text: Incorrect. Extension semantics are documented for tenant-ids under defaults; under override the list is replaced for the keyed job type.

c.text: Incorrect. The reference does not describe set intersection; override entries replace the annotation list of tenant IDs in 8.8.

d.text: Incorrect. Override entries are not silently dropped; they replace the annotation list for the worker registered against the keyed job type.

explanation: tenant-ids under camunda.client.worker.override.<job-type> replaces the annotation list of tenant IDs. Under camunda.client.worker.defaults the same property extends the annotation list instead.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
